title: Digital finance and green innovation: Evidence from China
abstract: This study examines the relationship between digital finance development and corporate green innovation using firm-level data from China. We find that digital finance significantly promotes green innovation, particularly in regions with better digital infrastructure.

title: Carbon pricing mechanisms in emerging economies: A comparative analysis
abstract: We compare carbon pricing mechanisms across major emerging economies, evaluating their effectiveness in reducing emissions while maintaining economic growth. Results show that hybrid systems combining carbon taxes with cap-and-trade achieve the best outcomes.

title: Artificial intelligence for climate change adaptation: Opportunities and risks
abstract: This review assesses the potential of AI technologies in climate change adaptation strategies. While AI offers significant opportunities for improving climate modeling and disaster prediction, we identify critical risks including algorithmic bias and energy consumption.

title: Green digital transformation and corporate carbon emissions
abstract: Using a quasi-natural experiment of China's digital economy pilot zones, we investigate the impact of digital transformation on corporate carbon emissions. Digital transformation reduces emissions through improved energy efficiency and innovation.

title: Blockchain technology for carbon trading: A systematic review
abstract: This paper systematically reviews the application of blockchain technology in carbon trading markets. We identify key advantages including transparency, traceability, and automation through smart contracts, while noting scalability challenges.

title: Digital economy and regional green development in China
abstract: This study explores the spatial spillover effects of digital economy development on regional green development efficiency. Results indicate significant positive spillover effects, with the digital economy promoting green development in both local and neighboring regions.

title: Smart city pilots and urban sustainability: Evidence from a global dataset
abstract: We evaluate the impact of smart city pilot programs on urban sustainability indicators across 150 cities worldwide. Smart city initiatives significantly improve energy efficiency and air quality, with effects varying by income level and governance quality.

title: Digital monitoring and environmental compliance: Field evidence
abstract: This field experiment examines the effect of digital monitoring technologies on firm compliance with environmental regulations. Real-time digital monitoring reduces violations by 30% compared to traditional inspection methods.
